sample.title: Findings Report: Sitter Performance Score Interviews
sample.author: Olivia Zinn
sample.excerpt: Summary Sitter comprehension of the metrics is basic and sometimes incorrect. There were issues with understanding how the metrics were calculated and correctly identifying what would make it go up or down. At the most basic level, they could often identify what impacted the metric (i.e. booking rate is based off whether or not a client books with them). There was evidence that terms like “first time request” don’t make sense and complicate comprehension. The biggest offender here is the…

sample.title: Red Routes
sample.author: Matteo Giuzzi (Unlicensed)
sample.excerpt: What are Red Routes Generally speaking, Red Routes are urban clearways that prohibit vehicles from stopping on the most crowded hi ghways , in a bid to prevent traffic jams from forming or at least minimize congestion resulting from lane blockages. The goal of red routes is to allow high traffic volumes to flow freely without obstruction . When applied to a digital products, Red routes are the critical and frequent paths that users take to complete their tasks. These routes are the foundational…

sample.title: Design exploration: unified modification model for ongoing relationships
sample.author: Bernardo Prudêncio
sample.excerpt: none Why this exploration exists Rover's recurring billing system was built around a weekly template — a fixed pattern that defines which days and times repeat, materialized into concrete units for a rolling 5-week window. That architecture made sense for a simple "same walk, same time, every week" use case, but it has become the root cause of most of the pain documented in the recurring relationships audit. The template model breaks down in a few predictable ways. Modifications are split…

sample.title: Upwork
sample.author: Lizeth Herrera
sample.excerpt: Summary Upwork is a global marketplace that connects businesses with freelancers and agencies across more than 10,000 skills. In 2025, the company’s revenue model evolved from a simple percentage-based fee into a multi‑pronged pay‑to‑play system. It charges freelancers variable commission fees (0–15 %), sells tokens called Connects to submit proposals, offers subscription plans that enhance visibility and analytics, collects processing and contract‑initiation fees from clients, and sells…

sample.title: Bookings on calendar: write Up
sample.author: Bernardo Prudêncio
sample.excerpt: New sitters The Good Most participants were able to correctly interpret the meanings of the color coding of the Red (fully booked - at capacity) and Grey/Patterned (unavailable) marked days on the calendar. For the most part, blank days were interpreted to be available days with no activity. Most participants discovered the “Select multiple day” and used it to complete tasks. Some participants tried a dragging motion to select contiguous days but easily self-corrected to pick a range of dates…

sample.title: Rate ordering in ledgers and settings
sample.author: Bernardo Prudêncio
sample.excerpt: none Context This proposal stems from our ongoing work around revenue optimization and user experience for both sitters and pet parents. Our current approach to displaying rates, does not reflect how customers naturally perceive or interact with different charges. Specifically, the current structure groups together different types of rates, such as optional add-ons (e.g:. bathing/grooming) with intrinsic booking charges (e.g:. 60 min, weekends). This can create confusion for users, as it blurs…

sample.title: Findings: SSS Safety Quiz
sample.author: Aaron Mitchell
sample.excerpt: View the maze report here See my manual analysis each tester here Takeaway The quiz takes 1.5 minutes to complete on average and 90% rated it as very easy. 85% of users passed the test, with 70% getting all 4 questions correct. 95% of users found the gifs very helpful, and only 1 user returned to the learning materials for reference during the quiz. Pet owners generally knew ‘most of the material, but learned a few new things’, while non pet owners generally ‘knew some of it but learned many…

sample.title: Findings: HVS early experiences and characteristics
sample.author: Olivia Zinn
sample.excerpt: Identification of two segments As part of this study , it became apparent there were two rough segments emerging from the “high volume” boarding group we identified (see criteria defined here ). I’m going to refer to the two groups as “moderate-earning full-time sitters” and “high-earning full-time sitters”. While they share a number of characteristics, there are also distinctions between them I want to call out. #DEEBFF The moderate-earning FT group refers to high-volume boarding providers who…

sample.title: Kibble Design System: Shadow Elevation Guide for UI Elements - WIP
sample.author: Karolina Kopacz
sample.excerpt: How and when to use Kibble elevation styles: elevation_low, elevation_medium, and elevation_high Overview Shadows in Kibble communicate vertical hierarchy - how far above the base surface a UI element is floating. Using them consistently across Rover helps users build an accurate mental model of what is interactive, what is temporary, and what demands immediate attention. There are three elevation tokens. Each maps to a specific layer in Rover's UI stack: Token Elevation level Layer in Rover UI…
